title: FAMILY CAREGIVER SUPPORT PROGRAM - 211info
text: MAIN OFFICE FAMILY CAREGIVER SUPPORT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about available services, assistance in accessing these services, individual counseling, support groups, and caregiver training. Respite care and supplemental services are available on a limited basis. 211 SE Byers Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Unpaid caregivers who meet one of the following: -Adult family members or other informal caregivers providing care to individuals 60 years of age and older -Adult family members or other informal caregivers providing care to individuals of any age with Alzheimer’s disease and related disorders -Older relatives (not parents) age 55 and older, providing care to children under the age of 18 -Older relatives, including parents, age 55 and older Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-5pm, Friday 8am-noon (541) 276-1926 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 276-1926 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call, or visit website for more information. Support group information is available by phone or online FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Gilliam, Hood River, Morrow, Sherman, Umatilla, Wasco, and Wheeler Counties TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Enter from the front lobby doors on SE Byers Avenue, south side of the building. Services: Adoption and Foster/Kinship Care Support Groups Caregiver Counseling Caregiver Training * Kinship Caregivers Caregiver/Care Receiver Support Groups Family Caregiver Subsidies Respite Care Subsidies Specialized Information and Referral * Kinship Caregivers Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - AREA AGENCY ON AGING COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY FOOD - HOME DELIVERED MEALS HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - AGING AND DISABILITY RESOURCE CONNECTION HOUSING/SHELTER - HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM HOUSING/SHELTER - WEATHERIZATION SERVICES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OPTIONS COUNSELING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE – MEDICAID INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES MATERIAL GOODS - HELPING HAND MONEY MANAGEMENT - CAPECO MONEY MANAGEMENT UTILITIES - UTILITY ASSISTANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. MAIN OFFICE
